briefs.title: Burnaby updates its official fee schedule — costs for city services may have changed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has formally approved Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, which updates the city's consolidated fees and charges bylaw — the official list of what businesses and residents pay for city permits, licences, and services.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any permits or licences you pay for have changed in cost.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby updates how bylaw notices are enforced — rules now in effect
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has formally passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating the rules around how bylaw notices (official warnings or fines from the city) are issued and enforced.
briefs.action_required: Check with Burnaby's bylaw office to confirm how notice delivery or dispute rules may affect your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates rules for parking meters and EV charging meters
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment updating its Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw. The changes affect how parking and EV charging meters are regulated in the city.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw rules.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Zoning change proposed for 2209 & 2225 Springer Ave — early council approval granted
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning amendment (a change to land-use rules) for two properties on Springer Avenue. The bylaw must pass further readings before it becomes law.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near 2209–2225 Springer Ave, monitor future council readings for final approval.

briefs.title: Zoning change at 5058 Imperial Street passes first two readings despite council opposition
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved the first two readings of a zoning amendment (a change to land-use rules) for 5058 Imperial Street, though four council members voted against it. The bylaw must still pass a third reading before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor council meetings for the third reading vote.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project officially initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has received the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan — a long-term blueprint guiding how land is used and developed across the city.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby's OCP process for upcoming public consultations or zoning changes that may affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Zoning change at 6800 Lougheed Highway moves forward after first two council readings
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning amendment (a change to the rules about how land can be used) for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway. The bylaw must pass further readings before it becomes law.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near or at 6800 Lougheed Hwy, monitor future council readings for the final zoning decision.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan and provincial funding push underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an action plan with community partners. The Mayor will also lobby the provincial government for funding to support the strategy.

briefs.title: City investment portfolio performance reviewed; 2026 forecast presented to council
briefs.summary: City council received a year-end report on its 2025 investment portfolio and a forecast for 2026. This is an information update only — no new rules or requirements were introduced.

briefs.title: Five-storey self-storage facility approved for rezoning at 2757 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how land can be used) for a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. No public hearing is needed because the project fits the city's existing Official Community Plan.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 3900 Grand Promenade rezoning adds 362 homes and hotel use, drops office space
briefs.summary: Council approved changes to an existing development plan at 3900 Grand Promenade, swapping office space for a hotel and restaurant, and adding 362 new market condos across two towers. Affordable housing units originally planned for this site will now be built at a nearby location called the Carrigan site.
briefs.action_required: If you own or plan a business near 3900 Grand Promenade, review the rezoning bylaw for impacts on your location or lease.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: 12-Storey, 290-Unit Mixed-Use Building Approved for 6535 Kingsway in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw for a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway, adding 290 residential units and ground-floor commercial space.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the rezoning bylaw process if your business operates near or at 6535 Kingsway.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Municipal asphalt paving contract worth $1.155M renewed with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving services, valued at $1,155,000 including taxes. No new rules or regulations were introduced.

briefs.title: Carrigan Court Mixed-Use Development Rezoning Application Accepted by Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has approved staff to accept a rezoning application for 3856–3870 Carrigan Court, allowing a high-density mixed-use building with commercial space and both strata (owner-owned units) and rental housing to move forward.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Carrigan Court, monitor rezoning progress for potential impacts on the area.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts with payment tied to actual quantities
briefs.summary: Council approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Contractors will be paid based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology, taxes included.

briefs.title: Modular four-plex pilot project contract sent back to staff for efficiency review
briefs.summary: City council paused the contract award for a modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue, sending it back to staff to find ways to manage the project more efficiently.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in 2026 membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities, the provincial association that represents local governments.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026–2030 Strategic Plan sets affordable housing priorities for five years
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the Burnaby Housing Authority's five-year strategic plan, which will guide how the city-owned affordable housing body operates and sets priorities through 2030.
